topics: Introduction to Signals and Systems: Elementary Signals, Classification and Representation of Continuous Time and Discrete Time Signals, Signal Operations, Concept of System, Continuous Time and Discrete Time Systems

Properties of Systems: Time Invariance, Linearity, Causality, Systems with and without Memory, Stability, Convolution Integral and Convolution Sum (Graphical and any One Matrix Method), Impulse and Step Response
Frequency Domain Characterization of Signals and Systems: Fourier Transform, Existence and Properties of Continuous Time Fourier Transform

Significance of Fourier Transform and Difference from Fourier Series: Energy Spectral Density and Power Spectral Density, Characterization of LTI Systems, Differential Equation Representation of Continuous Time LTI Systems, Transfer Function Representation of Differential Equation in Laplace Domain, Modeling of LTI Systems, Electrical and Translational Mechanical System Transfer Function Model
Sampled Data Systems and Z-Transform: Sampling Process
Impulse Train Sampling, Sampling Theorem, Aliasing Effect, Zero-Order and First-Order Hold Circuits

Signal Reconstruction, Z-Transform, Stability and Causality Conditions using ROC, Characterization of Difference Equations using Z-Transform, Pulse Transfer Function, Impulse Response of Discrete-Time Systems
Sampled Data System Representation and Fourier Analysis
Delay Operator and Block Diagram Representation: Direct Form
Cascade and Parallel Representations, Discrete Fourier Series
Fourier Representation of Discrete Time Signals: Discrete Fourier Series Properties, Discrete Time Fourier Transform
Properties and Frequency Response of Simple DT Systems
